/cwe/weakness/791.Name: Incomplete Filtering of Special Elements
/cwe/weakness/791.Description: The product receives data from an upstream component, but does not completely filter special elements before sending it to a downstream component.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: REALIZATION: This weakness is caused during implementation of an architectural security tactic.

Entries.IntroText: The following code takes untrusted input and uses a regular expression to filter "../" from the input. It then appends this result to the /home/user/ directory and attempts to read the file in the final resulting path.

Entries.BodyText: Since the regular expression does not have the /g global match modifier, it only removes the first instance of "../" it comes across. So an input value such as:
will have the first "../" stripped, resulting in:
This value is then concatenated with the /home/user/ directory:
which causes the /etc/passwd file to be retrieved once the operating system has resolved the ../ sequences in the pathname. This leads to relative path traversal (CWE-23).
